Preston Elmer Robert Villarreal 


We've noticed that temper seems to fluctuate if there's any pause in flow of product. When we reduced MPS the other day to 2050, we saw a positive result. Watching this morning, even at 2050, there were several times the flow of chocolate into the hopper stopped because it was full. I made an additional decrease down to 2030. Hopper is staying full, and our flow is more continuous. Seeing a steady trend on temper results since without any other adjustments. Additionally, our parity between setpoint and actual seems to have improved as well.
Maintenance is on the line adjusting the sensors because the C-6 belt is malfunctioning.
Adjusted the sensitivity on sensors  WB102, WB130 & WB140 to keep C6 running smoothly.

Preston Elmer 


Raised target pre-cooling by .2 so it doesn't drop below 91 too far.  It's actively regulating well right now
Good work making adjustments 👏

As long as it doesn't go too long in one direction - sending to hopper or sending to buffer. 
Typically we'll want to see about 30 seconds filling the hopper and up to 2 minutes sending to buffer. 
This prevents us from getting a mass pump fault because of chocolate freezing up in the pipes. 
Seems to be going well for now though 👍
Please reach out to me if you have questions on adjustments

We'll continue doing tests periodically for the remainder of the shift.

After force-starting the 2nd compressor, I waited until the temperature dropped low enough for both compressors to turn off, then I watched to see at what point they would both turn on again. When the temperature reached 1.0 degree C, the first compressor kicked on, and the second compressor kicked on almost immediately after at 1.1 degrees C. I would say that's typical for how the chiller normally runs. The issue is when the 2nd compressor never turns on, and the temperatures slowly begin to climb. There's no alarm or indication to the cause. So if the operators aren't vigilantly watching the primary glycol temperature, we're caught off guard with melted chocolate exiting Tunnel 2 and a mess of chocolate buildup all over the Matrix area. Curt Willis

Huh, almost like it was originally designed that way and then the order of lines moves got all messed up and line 1 matrix were where this was supposed to go all at the wrong time and we had to pivot to a short term solution 🤔

I tightened them up as best I could, but it looks like we used pipe tape or thread sealant on compression fittings when it was first installed. With these types of fittings, you don't generally need anything other than the fitting itself since it creates the seal. I found a fair few other compression fittings for the SS tubing in SL4 kitchen that have mineral build-up caused by the same issue.
